title: Interest Rate Statistics
summary: This is a U.S. Treasury landing page that describes various Treasury interest rate data series (par yield curves, real yield curves, T-bill rates, long-term rates) and their methodology. The page explains that yield data is derived from closing market bid prices obtained by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York at approximately 3:30 PM each business day. No actual rate data or current macro information is provided on this page.
key_points: The page documents Treasury yield curve methodology but does not provide actual yield data
Par yield curve rates are based on closing market bid prices of recently auctioned Treasury securities
Par real yields are derived from TIPS (Treasury Inflation Protected Securities) in the over-the-counter market
The Federal Reserve Bank of New York provides indicative quotations at approximately 3:30 PM daily
Data series include: Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates, Par Real Yield Curve Rates, T-Bill Rates, and Long-Term Rates
Treasury resumed publishing the 30-year constant maturity series on February 9, 2006 after ceasing on February 18, 2002
The Long-Term Real Rate Average series began January 2, 2004
why_it_matters: This page describes the methodology and data sources for Treasury yield data, which is fundamental to fixed income pricing and economic signaling. However, this specific page is documentation, not the underlying data itself. It does not provide current market rates or actionable macro information.
market_structure_context: The page describes the market structure of Treasury price discovery: data is collected from the over-the-counter market via the Federal Reserve Bank of New York at a fixed daily time (3:30 PM). This establishes the timing and methodology for benchmark Treasury price discovery.

issues_found: This is a documentation/landing page listing data series, not a research document or data release
No actual rate data or current macro figures are provided
Extremely thin content - just describes what data series exist and basic methodology
Not actionable as a standalone research archive item

notes: Low-value source for research archive. This is a navigation/documentation page that merely describes Treasury interest rate data series and their methodology. It does not provide actual yield data, current rates, or any actionable macro information. The content is institutional documentation, not a data release or research finding. Source is technically related to macro catalysts (yield curves are macro-relevant) but the page itself contains no macro data to archive.
